hypothesis: If combining pre-training on large chemical databases with domain-specific fine-tuning and expert-guided reinforcement learning improves the generation of valid, novel, and chemically appropriate covalent triazine framework structures compared to using any single knowledge transfer strategy alone, then a multi-stage transfer learning pipeline (pre-training → fine-tuning → reinforcement learning) will yield higher scores across validity, novelty, internal diversity, and expert filter passage rate metrics than models employing only pre-training or fine-tuning, because sequential knowledge transfer progressively constrains the chemical space toward structurally and functionally relevant CTF candidates for supercapacitor applications.
